scope: Approved pause action for Tracking Audit Nurture only.
userApproval: User explicitly approved pausing only the Tracking Audit Nurture workflow.
safetyBoundaries: Only Tracking Audit Nurture was paused.
No other workflow was paused, activated, edited, duplicated, deleted, renamed, or rebuilt.
No triggers, steps, delays, email templates, suppression rules, exit conditions, contacts, lists, CRM records, templates, campaigns, forms, sender settings, or attributes were edited.
No email was sent or resent.
No GA4, GTM, Meta, Google Ads, Netlify, Vercel settings, GitHub issue, or Notion state was touched.
Sensitive identifiers are redacted or omitted.

actionTaken.reason: The alternative Deactivate automation option stated that active contacts would be removed. User instructions did not approve removing contacts from the workflow.
actionTaken.activeContactHandlingDecision: User declined removing or stopping already-active contacts from the automation and asked to finalize documentation.

activeContactsView: Three redacted contacts were still visible in Contacts in workflow.
Two redacted contacts remained visible in Tracking Audit Nurture, at step 3 and step 9.
One redacted contact remained visible in General Enquiry Workflow at step 3.

importantLimitation: Brevo's pause option stated that no new contacts can enter the automation but active contacts will continue. Because the user declined removing or stopping already-active contacts, this action prevents new Tracking Audit Nurture entries but may not stop already-active contacts from continuing.
recommendedNextAction: Do not reactivate Tracking Audit Nurture before deeper workflow/template/suppression review and explicit approval. Do not submit more Tracking Audit leads while already-active contact behavior remains unresolved.
